extraction.one_line_summary: VERNON Ethan won Stage 5 sprint while ALMEIDA João took race lead by one second via third place at 17km-to-go intermediate sprint bonus seconds
tactical_patterns: Almeida/UAE prioritized intermediate sprint at 17km over stage finish, gaining crucial third place bonus while Quintana finished fourth without seconds
Quick-Step delivered perfect leadout despite depleted sprint field from illness, Vernon exploiting reduced competition from absent Hoffa/Matthews/De Vrient/Milano/Colbrelli
Johannesson's Astana contested intermediate sprint for GC gain rather than protecting energy, signaling aggressive tactics when on equal time

rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: UAE executed precise intermediate sprint positioning at 17km despite wearing young rider jersey, not leader's jersey, showing tactical awareness
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nacer: negative: failed to secure bonus seconds at crucial 17km intermediate despite attempting to duke it out, showing inferior sprint positioning
rider_signals.VERNON Ethan: positive: delivered decisive sprint after 5h20m in saddle on 206km stage following two mountain days, revealing unexpected endurance capacity
rider_signals.DENNIS Rowan: neutral: drove pace at 2.7km but Deceuninck-Quick-Step absent from his leadout, suggesting Ineos protected Omen rather than sprint support
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: negative: forced solo into final roundabouts with no leadout, showing team domestique depletion likely from illness
